summary: Static review (no dotnet SDK) of auto-login-after-verify fix in AccountController.VerifyEmail POST. All 7 verification points pass.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix is correctly applied at AccountController.cs lines 353-403. Order matches spec: SaveChangesAsync -> pendingStore.Remove -> TempData SuccessMessage (Freebie/WithPromo/PromoInvalidFreebie based on bonusGranted & pending.PromoCode state) -> Session.SetString UserEmail (line 384) -> Session.SetString JustLoggedIn=1 (line 385) -> Clinic branch (case-insensitive) with ClinicId cache via AsNoTracking + RedirectToAction Index/Dashboard area=CAM (lines 387-399) -> B2C fallback RedirectToAction Upload/Interpretation (line 403).

Old buggy line `TempData["ActiveTab"]="login"; return RedirectToAction("Index","Home");` no longer exists in VerifyEmail POST success path. Remaining `ActiveTab=login` occurrences (lines 454,466,475,592,642) are inside Login/ForgotPassword actions - correct and unrelated.
Error paths at lines 193, 200, 219, 229 correctly still redirect to Home/Index (unauth failure states preserved).
`using Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore;` present at line 5 - AsNoTracking()/FirstOrDefaultAsync compile.

InterpretationController.Upload GET at line 61 confirmed: reads Session UserEmail (line 63), loads user (line 66), gate at line 73 checks `CreditRest<=0 && BonusCreditsRemaining<=0` -> fresh B2C user with BonusCredits=1/BonusCreditsConsumed=0 passes cleanly and view renders.
CAM Dashboard route exists: /app/MedicalApp/Areas/CAM/Controllers/DashboardController.cs has [Area("CAM")] on class (line 14) and public async Task<IActionResult> Index (line 40).

Minor observation (not blocking): ClinicId cache uses `if (clinicId > 0)` - safe since default int is 0 when no clinic row found; the DB row was just added in same SaveChangesAsync so should always be >0 for Clinic userType path.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 7 - static review only (no dotnet SDK in sandbox). User validates at runtime on VS2026. Fix verified across all 7 spec bullets. No code modified.
